title: Policy for the responsible use of AI in government – AI Transparency Statement | Office of the Special Investigator

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.text: The Office of the Special Investigator (OSI) receives Information Technology (IT) services from the Attorney-General’s Department (AGD) and the Australian Federal Police (AFP) under shared services agreements. The use of artificial intelligence (AI) by the OSI is in line with the AI policies of AGD and AFP.

sections.heading: Data privacy and security
sections.text: Protecting the privacy and security of individuals' data, sensitive and classified information is of paramount importance to us. When using AI we will ensure that any data used is handled in accordance with the Privacy Act 1988, the Protective Security Policy Framework and other relevant data protection policy for the responsible use of AI in government.

sections.heading: AI governance and oversight
sections.text: We have appointed an Accountable Official in accordance with Policy for the responsible use of AI in government. Oversight of any AI project will be done by governance bodies within the OSI.

sections.heading: Continuous improvement
sections.text:
The OSI will regularly review and update AI policies and practices in consultation with our shared services partners. This includes staying informed about new developments in AI technology, ethics, and regulatory policy for the responsible use of AI in government.
This statement will be reviewed annually, when we make a significant change to our approach to AI, or when new factors impact this statement.
AI training is available to all staff.

sections.heading: Contact information
sections.text:
If you have questions or would like more information, please contact us at:
Contact us | Office of the Special Investigator.
Mailing Address : GPO Box 812 Canberra ACT 2601.
